column6: Description
column8: Website function: suggest study spots and recommend music based on criteria: noise level, location, etc

column6: Nice and tucked away, good to work with a small group of friends. Sometimes this spot gets snapped up fast.
column8: Want user to be able to sort and filter spots by location (indoor/outdoor), on/off campus, distance from campus, noise level

column6: Good to do readings or light work outside with friends.
column8: Maybe rank focus level on a scale? 1 = casual study hang out with friends, 4 = i don't want to be disturbed at all I really need to get stuff done

column6: Pretty wood bookshelves and details, cozy couches
column8: 1: looking to casually study with friends or work on a project in a group

column6: Lots of light, can be nice and warm in the winter because of the windows
column8: 2: Looking to study quietly with friends

column6: The bar seating by the window is nice
column8: 3: Looking to study quietly but I don't mind talking with people passing through

column6: Good for group projects.
column8: 4: I want a silent and more private space to study alone

column6: Nice individual desks, group tables, and armchairs
If you can get one, the booths are great for studying with friends. It can get pretty loud in here if it's busy so not the best if you don't have good headphones.
column8: Tags: based on major/ department, population/ business, type of assignment, capacity (how many seats)

column6: Big south-facing windows, nice in the winter to get some sunlight.
Good sunlight
Big pretty windows, gorgeous in the fall. No food allowed in here, don't bring snacks
Silent study by yourself, good for if you want to block out distractions.
Can get loud and busy around mealtimes, but the seats by the window are nice to work with outlets nearby– also great people-watching
Nice view of the river. Good to sit and study during office hours in case you get stuck.
Big window that looks right out over the LU Arch
Good variety of seating: booths, couches, and chairs/tables. Sometimes big groups in the booths can get loud.
Good food, can get v busy on weekends.
Seasonal, they take these away in the winter. Tables v wobbly but nice when it's nice out
Math tutoring here, nice group tables
Good place to get food, outdoor patio on the restaurant part is v nice
Lots of little group tables, fantastic place to lock in if empty. Physics, bio, and chem tutoring held here.
Math and computer science tutoring here
Basically an empty classroom. Good for quiet homework with a couple friends
Cool booths and stuff here and nice and quiet
Awesome windows with a nice view of the river
Very nice view of the river.

column1: Memorial Hall Diversity Equity & Inclusion Center
column6: Awesome couches and very cozy in the winter
Little rooms that require ID access, good for if you need total quiet
Limited spots, but there's a couple of nice group tables. Level A has the periodicals collection, so it's generally nice and quiet.
Beautiful hiking/walking trails, and indoor study spaces in "The Lodge"
Recently renovated, really nice new spaces, and some good private meeting rooms.
Nice couches and wingback armchairs.
Nice to study in a "house house"
Comfy and social, good place to study between classes/lessons in the con
Nice round table and some couches, typically pretty quiet. Smells like pool.
Usually quieter than the copper rock on College, booths are less comfy.
New!
It's a Starbucks
A little farther off campus
Hidden Seth's coffee!
